title: IISc Administrative Assistant - Platinum Guess Paper
instructions: This is a Platinum quality guess paper modelled strictly after the 2023 official paper.

questions.text: The intersection point of the lines 3x - 2y = 12 and 5x + y = 33 lies on which of the following lines?
A circular track passes through all three vertices of a triangular field with side lengths 52 m, 56 m, and 60 m. What is the radius of the circular track?
By selling an article at 75% of its marked price, a trader makes a loss of 10%. If he sells it at 90% of its marked price, what will be his profit or loss percentage?

Ravi spends 60% of his monthly income on household items and 25% of the remaining on transport. If his income is increased by 20% and his household expenditure is increased by 10%, but his transport expenditure remains the same, what is the percentage change in his savings?

A and B together can do a piece of work in 12 days. B's efficiency is 25% less than C's efficiency. A, B, and C together can do the same work in 8 days. In how many days can A alone complete 2/3 of the work?
Let x be the greatest number which when divides 415, 627, and 945 leaves the same remainder in each case. What is the sum of the digits of x?
A sum of money invested at simple interest amounts to Rs. 8,400 in 3 years and to Rs. 9,600 in 5 years. What is the principal sum?
Five years ago, the ratio of the ages of A and B was 4:5. Eight years from now, the ratio of their ages will be 17:20. What is the ratio of A's present age to B's age 4 years ago?

The price of a laptop is 120% more than the price of a tablet. If the price of the laptop is increased by 15% and the price of the tablet is decreased by 10%, what is the percentage increase in the total cost of buying 2 laptops and 3 tablets?

Pipe A can fill a tank in 18 hours and Pipe B in 24 hours. Pipe C can empty the full tank in 36 hours. If all three pipes are opened together for 4 hours, and then Pipe A is closed, how many more hours will it take to fill the remaining tank?

A bucket is in the shape of a frustum of a cone with radii of its circular ends being 28 cm and 21 cm, and its depth is 30 cm. Water flows into the bucket through a cylindrical pipe of radius 1.4 cm at a speed of 50 cm/sec. How many seconds will it take to fill the empty bucket completely? (Take pi = 22/7)
A retailer marks up his goods by 40% above the cost price and allows a discount of 15%. If he earns a profit of Rs. 475, what is the marked price of the goods?

A, B, and C started a business with initial investments in the ratio 3:4:5. After 4 months, A doubled his investment, and after another 4 months, B halved his investment. If the total annual profit is Rs. 14,800, what is C's share?

The time taken by a boat to travel a certain distance upstream is 7/4 times the time taken to travel the same distance downstream. If the speed of the current is 3 km/hr, what is the speed of the boat in still water?

Alloy A contains copper and zinc in the ratio 5:3. Alloy B contains copper and zinc in the ratio 3:4. In what ratio should Alloys A and B be mixed to get a new alloy containing equal amounts of copper and zinc?

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom:
"To tilt at windmills"

options.text: To waste time on trivial matters

questions.text:
Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the sentence.
No sooner had she finished the speech [then the audience gave] a standing ovation.

Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.
A person who collects or has a great love of books

Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order.
A. The phenomenon of quantum entanglement
B. states that two particles can be intimately linked
C. to understand the fundamental nature of the universe
D. independent of the distance separating them

Select the most appropriate synonym of the bracketed word to fill in the blank.
The committee's (ephemeral) popularity was largely due to the crisis at hand.
Select the correctly spelt word.

Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the sentence.
He warned the workers that [unless they do not complete] the project on time, they will be fired.

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom:
"To bite the bullet"

options.text: To force yourself to do something unpleasant
To get injured in a conflict
To make a tough decision blindly

questions.text:
Select the most appropriate antonym of the bracketed word to fill in the blank.
Despite the (cacophony) in the market, she managed to focus on her reading.

Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A. However, this is changing rapidly as renewable energy becomes cheaper.
B. For decades, coal has been the dominant source of electricity generation.
C. Solar and wind power are leading this global transition.
D. Consequently, many countries are phasing out coal power plants entirely.
Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.

Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.
A person who is indifferent to both pleasure and pain

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom:
"Cut to the chase"

options.text: To get to the main point

questions.text:
Read the passage and answer the question:
The transition to a circular economy represents a fundamental shift in how we produce and consume goods. Unlike the traditional linear model—take, make, dispose—a circular economy aims to design out waste, keep materials in use, and regenerate natural systems. This approach not only mitigates environmental degradation but also offers substantial economic benefits by reducing supply chain risks and fostering innovation in product design.

According to the passage, what is a primary goal of the circular economy?

options.text: To design out waste and keep materials in use
To increase the consumption of disposable goods
To traditionalize the linear economic model
To eliminate the need for product innovation

questions.text:
Read the passage in the previous question and answer:
What is the central theme of the passage?

options.text: The shift towards sustainable and regenerative economic practices
The inevitable decline of global supply chains
The environmental dangers of technological innovation
The historical evolution of linear economies

questions.text:
Read the passage in the previous questions and answer:
Which of the following is closest in meaning to the word 'mitigates' as used in the passage?

Select the figure that will come next in the following figure series.

[Figure Placeholder: A series of geometric shapes rotating 90 degrees clockwise with an alternating shaded quadrant]

Select the correct mirror image of the given combination when the mirror is placed at MN as shown.

[Figure Placeholder: Alphanumeric string 'KL97Q']

Two different positions of the same dice are shown, the six faces of which are numbered from 1 to 6. Select the number that will be on the face opposite to the face having the number '5'.

[Figure Placeholder: Dice positions showing (1, 4, 5) and (1, 2, 6)]

Select the option figure that will complete the pattern in the given figure.

[Figure Placeholder: A grid with a missing corner containing intersecting diagonals]
In a certain code language, 'ROBUST' is written as 'QNATRS'. How will 'ZEBRA' be written in that language?
In a certain code language, 'PAINT' is coded as '60', and 'DROP' is coded as '53'. How will 'MARK' be coded in that language?

Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.
14, 21, 35, 56, 84, ?

Select the number that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.
5, 11, 25, 55, 117, ?

Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F, and G are sitting in a straight line facing north. B sits third to the left of G. G sits at one of the extreme ends. A sits second to the right of C. C is not an immediate neighbour of B. E and F are immediate neighbours. E is not sitting at any extreme end. Who sits at the extreme left end?

Six friends P, Q, R, S, T, and U are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. P sits second to the right of U. Q sits immediate left of R. S is not an immediate neighbour of P. Who sits opposite to T?
Pointing to a photograph, a man says, "She is the only daughter of my wife's mother-in-law." How is the woman in the photograph related to the man?

If A + B means A is the brother of B; A - B means A is the sister of B; A * B means A is the father of B; A / B means A is the mother of B.
Which of the following expressions shows that P is the paternal aunt of R?

Statements:
1. All cars are buses.
2. Some buses are trucks.
3. No truck is a bicycle.
Conclusions:
I. Some cars are trucks.
II. No bus is a bicycle.
III. Some buses are cars.
Among five friends J, K, L, M, and N, each has a different height. J is taller than only L. K is taller than M but shorter than N. Who is the third tallest?

Select the option that is related to the third letter-cluster in the same way as the second letter-cluster is related to the first.
GATE : HCYI :: SINK : ?
Four letter-clusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the odd letter-cluster.

Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series will complete the series.
a _ c d _ b _ d a b c _
If all the vowels are dropped from the string 'ALPHANUMERIC', which letter will be the fourth from the right end?

Which two numbers should be interchanged to make the given equation correct?
14 + 5 × 4 - 12 ÷ 3 = 26

What approximate value will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
24.98 × 15.02 - 110.11 + 39.99 = ?

A man is facing West. He turns 45 degrees in the clockwise direction and then another 180 degrees in the same direction, and then 270 degrees in the anti-clockwise direction. Which direction is he facing now?

Seven lectures are scheduled on seven different days of the same week starting from Monday to Sunday. Lecture P is on Thursday. Two lectures are between P and Q. R is scheduled immediately before Q. Three lectures are between R and S. On which day is S scheduled?
Who among the following was the founder of the 'Servants of India Society' in 1905?
The system of Dagh (branding of horses) and Chehra (descriptive roll of soldiers) was introduced by which Delhi Sultan?
Which institution replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015?
The Majuli island, which is the largest river island in the world, is located on which river?
The Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award, India's highest sporting honour, was formerly known as:
The famous 'Hornbill Festival' is celebrated in which state of India?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution guarantees the right to constitutional remedies?
Which of the following was India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory?
Who is the author of the book 'The Discovery of India'?
Which of the following Indian states does NOT share a border with Bangladesh?
In the Global Hunger Index 2023, India was ranked at which position out of 125 countries?
The Harappan site of Dholavira is located in which Indian state?
Which of the following is the central bank of India?
Which vitamin is also known as Ascorbic Acid?
Kuchipudi is a classical dance form of which Indian state?
Which country hosted the G20 Summit in the year 2023?
What will be the output of the function '=OR(A1>50, B1<10)' in MS-Excel 2010?

options.text: TRUE if either A1 is greater than 50 or B1 is less than 10
TRUE only if both conditions are met
FALSE if A1 is 60 and B1 is 5

questions.text: Which keyboard shortcut is used to create a new, blank workbook in MS-Excel?
Which type of memory is non-volatile and is used to store the computer's startup instructions (BIOS)?
Which of the following components is known as the 'brain' of the computer?
Disk defragmenter is an example of which type of software?
What does the keyboard shortcut 'Windows Key + L' do in Windows 10?

Which of the following statements is/are true?
(i) Open-source software provides access to its source code.
(ii) Freeware and open-source software are exactly the same thing.

Identify whether the given statements are true or false.
a) Computers possess artificial intelligence inherently without programming.
b) Computers can process millions of instructions per second without fatigue.
Which of the following is NOT an operating system?

What will be the output of the following MS-Excel formula?
= (8 / 2) * (2 + 2) + 3^2
